title: Neoporteria horrida

Specifically, Neoporteria horrida is part of the subfamily Opuntioideae, known for its distinctive, fleshy stems. The exact classification of Neoporteria horrida is still subject to ongoing research and possible revisions. However, it is widely accepted as a unique and fascinating member of the cactus family.

intro: Neoporteria horrida, also known as Proustia horrida, is a species of cactus in the family Cactaceae, native to the Atacama Desert in Chile. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including the treatment of wounds, inflammation, and pain relief due to its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The bioactive constituents of Neoporteria horrida include flavonoids, alkaloids, and glycosides, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
